rationale: Item 19 reports actual historic gross revenue from affiliate-owned outlets. The FPR explicitly adjusts for the same fee rates that a franchisee would pay: 6% royalty and local advertising (greater of 1.5% or $2,000/mo). The National Ad Fund (2%) is NOT deducted because it starts after 12 months — this is acknowledged. The Item 19 cohort uses the same royalty rate (6%) and same local advertising formula as current new franchisees per Item 6. No fee tier discrepancy exists between the FPR cohort economics and the current franchise offering terms.
note_on_ad_fund: The 2% NAF is not reflected in Item 19 adjustments because it starts after 12 months. This is disclosed. A franchisee's actual adjusted revenue in year 2+ would be approximately 2% lower than the Item 19 adjusted figures. This is a presentation note, not a cohort-comparability discrepancy.
scorecard_note: Item 19 cohort comparability: no discrepancy found. FPR uses same royalty rate (6%) as current franchisees. NAF (2%) exclusion from adjustment is disclosed.
